name: Static vs Dynamic Typing
def: Whether types are checked at compile time (static) or at run time (dynamic).
problem: Trade early error detection and tooling against flexibility and rapid iteration.
mechanism: Static type systems verify types before execution and erase or retain them at runtime; dynamic systems tag values and check on use.
benefits: Static: errors caught early, rich tooling, performance
Dynamic: flexibility, less ceremony, rapid prototyping
limits: Static: more upfront annotation; can fight the checker
Dynamic: type errors surface at runtime; weaker tooling
failures: Dynamic code shipping type bugs to production
whenNot: Static for tiny throwaway scripts; dynamic for large safety-critical systems
myths: "Static typing prevents all bugs" — it prevents a class of them, not logic errors
